decisions.summary: Continuous warm off-white surface across slides + results + footer. Main result card on white elevated with 3px orange left-border. Wedge + override callouts get 4–6px orange top-strip. Footer warm bg + orange top rule. Three orange section dividers on results page (above main card, line 601 boundary, above footer).

Warm-gray 10-stop ramp throughout. All 10 stops declared (50–900) even where 700/800/900 have no current Tailwind consumer (D-09 client-brandability). Keep slate-* utility name in markup (no rename per D-07).

Cat D indigo utilities map to Overdrive secondary palette by role (Light Yellow #FFE599 + Golden Yellow #F1C232). Warning icon = Golden Yellow #F1C232. Hovers derive from --accent-rgb via alpha (zero new hover tokens). +2 net-new tokens (--brand-soft-rgb, --brand-secondary-rgb); contract grows ~13 → ~15.
Family swap Fraunces → Space Grotesk + minimum-viable adjustments only where letterform clashes (browser-decided post-swap).

Browser-verify BEFORE plan locks AND after every <head>-touching execute task. Structural mitigation for V-4 lesson — generalized into a phase-level standard. VALIDATION.md must encode as hard verify gate rows.

next_action: Run /gsd-plan-phase 2 — produces RESEARCH, PATTERNS, PLAN, VALIDATION. The research phase MUST browser-verify any proposed <head> ordering or <script>/<link> change BEFORE PLAN.md locks (D-14 + HANDOFF.json blocking_constraints_for_phase_2[0]). Recommend /clear first for a fresh planning context.

blocking_constraints_for_phase_2.name: empirical-probes-on-head-element-ordering
blocking_constraints_for_phase_2.description: Phase 1 RESEARCH §5 prescribed a <head> ordering based on an unverified runtime-behavior claim ('CDN is defer-equivalent — reads window.tailwind.config when it executes, which is after the inline config script has set it'). Structurally checked via grep ordering assertions and plan-checker iter 2, but never browser-loaded. V-4 caught the runtime ReferenceError after 3 commits had landed. Cost: 1 fix commit (00a6b21) + 1 docs correction (a9e5b35). Phase 2 still touches <head> (Google Fonts <link> swap for Space Grotesk) and depends on font-loading runtime behavior — same risk surface.
blocking_constraints_for_phase_2.structural_mitigation: Phase 2 D-14 (in 02-CONTEXT.md): browser-verify BEFORE plan locks AND after every <head>-touching execute task. Research-phase: browser-load proposed <head> ordering via 'python3 -m http.server' + DevTools console; zero JS errors + visible identity matches baseline; BEFORE writing into PLAN.md. Execute-phase: same verification after every task that modifies <head>, adds/moves <script>/<link>, or depends on/mutates globals. VALIDATION.md encodes as hard verify gate rows, not single end-of-phase sweep. Grep-ordering assertions are necessary but NOT sufficient for runtime-behavior claims about <head> element ordering.

advisory_constraints.name: line-range-extrapolation-in-planning-artifacts
advisory_constraints.description: While writing 02-CONTEXT.md, three line-range claims were generated by extrapolating endpoints from earlier grep results that only showed range starts and adjacent sections — never grep-verifying each closing </style> / </script> at write-time. Errors: 'lines 13–83 token contract' (actual 13–84), 'line 84 CDN script' (actual line 85), 'lines 85–119 Tailwind config' (actual 86–123). Caught and corrected via grep-verify before commit (not shipped). CONTEXT.md is downstream agents' ground truth — they won't re-verify line numbers before writing PLAN.md / RESEARCH.md.
advisory_constraints.guidance: When writing line-anchored claims into a planning artifact (CONTEXT.md, RESEARCH.md, PLAN.md, SUMMARY.md), grep both the start AND end of each cited line range at write-time. Never extrapolate an endpoint from a neighboring section's start — extrapolated endpoints become silent drift the moment another agent edits the file. Lesson captured in ~/.claude/lessons.md (loaded at SessionStart for every session).

advisory_constraints.name: stale-codebase-maps-2026-03-05
advisory_constraints.description: Codebase maps in .planning/codebase/ (STACK.md, STRUCTURE.md, CONVENTIONS.md) are dated 2026-03-05 — pre-Phase-1. STACK.md mentions `primary` / `primaryHover` tokens and Inter font; Phase 1 D-05/D-07 changed both. CONVENTIONS.md describes pre-Phase-1 Tailwind config shape. STRUCTURE.md describes pre-Phase-1 file layout. Phase 2 researcher/planner MUST grep current index.html directly for tokens, utility usages, and line anchors — do NOT trust the maps for line numbers, token names, or current Tailwind config shape. Originally surfaced in Phase 1 (D-08 / V-4 lesson). Still active.
advisory_constraints.guidance: Treat .planning/codebase/ as pre-Phase-1 historical context only. Re-grep index.html at the start of every Phase 2 sub-step (research, plan, execute). Re-running /gsd-map-codebase after Phase 2 ships would refresh the maps for Phase 3, but is out of scope for Phase 2 itself.

advisory_constraints.name: claude-code-worktree-spawn-base-quirk
advisory_constraints.description: Claude Code's Agent(isolation='worktree') spawned the Phase 1 executor's worktree off main (04a9b0f) rather than off the orchestrator's HEAD (rebrand-theming @ e3a92fd). Executor self-corrected via 'git reset --hard rebrand-theming' before any commits — safe because the worktree branch had zero unique commits. If a future executor commits BEFORE doing the base-check, the harness can lose work.
advisory_constraints.guidance: Phase 2 executor agents should assert the worktree's expected base in their FIRST action (before any commits) and HALT if drift is detected. The gsd-executor agent prompt template already includes this check; trust it. If drift is detected at the wrong time (post-commit), do NOT self-recover — surface to the user for manual reconciliation.

context_notes: Phase 2 discuss complete; 14 decisions captured (D-01..D-14) in 02-CONTEXT.md. Net token-contract growth: +2 (--brand-soft-rgb, --brand-secondary-rgb). Retires --surface-dark-rgb + --surface-dark-card-rgb. Wires --surface-elev-rgb (Phase 1 declared with no consumer until now). 02-CONTEXT.md is canonical input for /gsd-plan-phase 2; 02-DISCUSSION-LOG.md is human audit trail (do NOT feed to research/planner/executor agents). HANDOFF.json blocking_constraints_for_phase_2 carries the V-4 lesson forward; 02-CONTEXT.md D-14 IS the structural mitigation; planner must encode browser-verify gates as hard VALIDATION.md rows. Branch rebrand-theming ahead 3 vs origin (d21fcd1, 75f8836, b2dd7e1) — push when convenient; not blocking next session. Pre-existing .planning/COACH-STATE.md remains untracked (not session-related).
